Vendingpreneur Glossary (A-Z)
How to Use the Vendingpreneurs Glossary
Modern Amenities
⚡️

This glossary is here to help you make sense of the language you’ll hear inside this community.

In vending, people often reference manufacturers, machine models, tools, financing options, and shorthand without stopping to explain them. This space gives you quick, plain-language context so you can follow conversations without feeling behind.

You don’t need to read this from top to bottom. Most members use the glossary as a reference when a term comes up and they want quick context.

On desktop, you can use your browser’s find tool (Command/Ctrl + F) to search within the glossary. On mobile, scroll through the alphabetized sections to find what you’re looking for. You can also use the Space search to explore related terms and content.

How to get the most out of it

Look up terms you hear on calls or see referenced in posts

Skim definitions to understand what something is and where it fits

Follow links when you want deeper context (for example, to VendHub or related spaces)

Some entries include simple labels (like financing, tools, or manufacturers) to help you understand what kind of thing you’re looking at. When relevant, you’ll also see links pointing you to where active partners, tools, or deeper discussions live elsewhere in the community.

This glossary isn’t meant to be exhaustive or static. It will grow as new terms, tools, and references come up — just like the business itself.

If you ever hear something you don’t recognize, this is a good place to start.

If you hear a term that should be added, you can suggest it in the Glossary Requests thread here.

How to Use the Vendingpreneurs Glossary
Vendingpreneur Glossary (A-Z)
How to Use the Vendingpreneurs Glossary
Vendingpreneur Glossary (A-Z)
Vendingpreneur Glossary (A-Z)
Modern Amenities
⚡️
#

365

A micromarket and smart vending platform that provides hardware, software, and payment systems for self-service retail locations.

Context: #Machines #Platforms #Operations
Where this shows up: When comparing micromarket or smart vending platforms
Related: Micromarket

A


Ambient vs cold

A way to describe whether a product is stored at room temperature (ambient) or in a refrigerated section (cold).

Context: #Machines #Merchandising
Where this shows up: When planning product mix or machine layout

B
C


Certificate of insurance (COI)

A document provided by an insurance company that proves a business has active insurance coverage and lists key policy details.

Context: #Operations #Compliance
Where this shows up: When a property requests proof of insurance

COGS

Cost of Goods Sold; the direct cost of purchasing the products that are sold through a machine.

Context: #Finance
Where this shows up: When calculating margins or profitability

Cost

The amount paid to acquire a product, service, or asset, before any markup or profit.

Context: #Finance
Where this shows up: When pricing products or evaluating expenses

CRM

Customer Relationship Management software used to track contacts, conversations, and follow-ups over time.

Context: #Sales #Systems
Where this shows up: When managing outreach or property relationships

D
E
F


Fit criteria

The set of characteristics used to evaluate whether a location is a good match for vending, such as traffic patterns, layout, and population.

Context: #Sales #Launching
Where this shows up: When filtering or evaluating potential locations

Foot traffic

The amount and pattern of people moving through a space as part of their normal routine.

Context: #Sales #Launching
Where this shows up: When evaluating location quality or placement zones

G


Garden-style layout

A property layout with multiple buildings or entrances spread across a site rather than a single centralized structure.

Context: #Sales #Launching
Where this shows up: When assessing how easily people pass a machine

Gross margin

Gross profit expressed as a percentage of revenue.

Context: #Finance
Where this shows up: When comparing profitability across products

Gross profit

Revenue minus the cost of goods sold.

Context: #Finance
Where this shows up: When evaluating product or machine performance

Gross revenue

Total sales before any costs, fees, or expenses are deducted.

Context: #Finance
Where this shows up: In sales reports and performance summaries

H


HaHa

A smart vending and micromarket platform offering connected machines and self-checkout technology.

Context: #Machines #Platforms
Where this shows up: When discussing machine or platform options

I
J
K


Kit / kitting

The process of grouping multiple products together to stock, transport, or service machines more efficiently.

Context: #Operations
Where this shows up: During picking or route preparation

L


LLC

Limited Liability Company; a business structure that separates personal and business liability.

Context: #Finance #Compliance
Where this shows up: When forming or describing a business entity

M


Margin

The difference between a product’s selling price and its cost, expressed either as dollars or percentage.

Context: #Finance
Where this shows up: When deciding what products to carry

Micromarket

An unattended self-service retail space with open shelving and self-checkout, typically larger than a vending machine.

Context: #Machines #Platforms
Where this shows up: When comparing vending formats

Micromart

A vending machine manufacturer known for smart vending units with large video screens and digital price displays.

Context: #Machines #Platforms
Where this shows up: When comparing smart vending hardware or machine features
Related: Micromart

MileIQ

Mileage-tracking software commonly used to log business driving for tax purposes.

Context: #Finance #Systems
Where this shows up: When tracking vehicle usage

Mock-up

A visual or conceptual representation of a proposed setup, placement, or layout.

Context: #Sales #Launching
Where this shows up: When explaining a plan to a property

MSA

Master Services Agreement; a contract that defines general terms governing a business relationship.

Context: #Sales #Compliance
Where this shows up: When formalizing agreements with properties

N


Net profit

Revenue minus all expenses, including product costs, fees, and operating expenses.

Context: #Finance
Where this shows up: When assessing true profitability

Net revenue

Revenue remaining after certain deductions, such as fees or revenue share, but before all expenses.

Context: #Finance
Where this shows up: In platform or processor reports

O


OpCo

A regional distribution center operated by Vistar that handles local fulfillment, deliveries, and account support for platforms like VendMarket.

Context: #Operations #SupplyChain
Where this shows up: When coordinating orders, deliveries, or working with a local distributor representative
Related: VendMarket

OptConnect

A cellular connectivity provider commonly used to connect vending and smart machines to the internet. Part of 365's normal machine setup.

Context: #Machines #Operations
Where this shows up: When discussing machine connectivity

P


Parlevel

A vending management software platform that provides machine data, reporting, inventory tracking, and operational tools for vending operators. Parlevel is now part of 365 Retail Markets.

Context: #Operations #Data #Systems
Where this shows up: When discussing machine reporting, inventory management, or backend analytics
Related: VMS

Pick list

A list of products and quantities needed to restock one or more machines.

Context: #Operations
Where this shows up: During route preparation

Planogram

A visual or written plan that shows how products are arranged within a machine or display.

Context: #Merchandising
Where this shows up: When standardizing layouts

POS

Point of Sale system; the hardware and software that processes transactions.

Context: #Machines #Operations
Where this shows up: When discussing payments or reporting

Price

The amount a customer pays for a product.

Context: #Finance
Where this shows up: On machines and in pricing discussions

Price sensitivity

How strongly customer demand changes in response to price changes.

Context: #Finance #Merchandising
Where this shows up: When adjusting prices

Price visibility

How clearly prices are displayed or seen by customers before purchase.

Context: #Merchandising
Where this shows up: When designing machine layouts

Processing fee

A fee charged by payment processors for handling card or app transactions.

Context: #Finance
Where this shows up: In monthly statements or reports

Product mix

The combination of products stocked in a machine or location.

Context: #Merchandising
Where this shows up: When launching or adjusting inventory

Profit First

A cash-management method that allocates profit before other expenses.

Context: #Finance #Mindset
Where this shows up: In discussions about financial discipline

Q
R


Revenue

Money collected from product sales.

Context: #Finance
Where this shows up: In sales and performance reporting

Revenue per machine (RPM)

Average revenue generated by a single machine over a given time period.

Context: #Finance #Operations
Where this shows up: When comparing machine performance

Revenue share

An agreement where a portion of revenue is paid to a property or partner.

Context: #Sales #Finance
Where this shows up: In contracts or negotiations

Resale license

A permit that allows a business to purchase products without paying sales tax upfront.

Context: #Compliance
Where this shows up: When ordering inventory (especially for the first time with a new supplier)

RFP

Request for Proposal; a formal document used to invite vendors to submit pricing and service details for a defined scope of work.

Context: #Sales #Compliance
Where this shows up: When a property or organization is comparing multiple vendors


S


SandStar

A micromarket and smart vending platform offering self-checkout retail solutions.

Context: #Machines #Platforms
Where this shows up: In platform comparisons


Shelf life

The length of time a product remains sellable before expiration or spoilage.

Context: #Merchandising #Operations
Where this shows up: When selecting products

Shrink

Inventory loss due to damage, theft, expiration, or error.

Context: #Operations #Finance
Where this shows up: When reconciling inventory

SKU

Stock Keeping Unit; a unique identifier for a specific product.

Context: #Operations #Data
Where this shows up: In ordering and reporting systems

Sole Proprietor

A business structure where one individual owns and operates the business.

Context: #Finance #Compliance
Where this shows up: When forming or describing a business

Stock

To place products into a machine or inventory location.

Context: #Operations
Where this shows up: During servicing

Stocker

A person responsible for restocking machines.

Context: #Operations
Where this shows up: In staffing or delegation discussions

Stockwell

A smart vending machine from 365 with both ambient and cold sides.

Context: #Machines #Platforms
Where this shows up: When choosing machine types

T


Tax-exempt

Status allowing certain purchases to be made without paying sales tax.

Context: #Compliance #Finance
Where this shows up: When ordering inventory

Telemetry

The automated transmission of machine data such as sales, inventory, or errors.

Context: #Machines #Data
Where this shows up: In monitoring and reporting

U


USG

Unified Strategies Group; a purchasing cooperative that provides vending operators access to negotiated pricing, rebates, and distributor programs through approved suppliers.

Context: #Finance #SupplyChain
Where this shows up: When discussing rebates, distributor pricing, or cooperative purchasing
Related: Unified Strategies Group (USG)

V


VMS

Vending Management System; software used to monitor sales, track inventory, manage routes, and analyze performance across vending machines or markets.

Context: #Operations #Data #Systems
Where this shows up: When comparing backend software platforms or evaluating operational tools

Velocity

The rate at which a product sells over time.

Context: #Merchandising #Data
Where this shows up: When deciding what to restock

W


Walkthrough

An in-person visit to observe a location’s layout, traffic, and conditions, often conducted with a representative of the property.

Context: #Sales #Launching
Where this shows up: When evaluating a property

X
Y
Z

*

If you hear a term that should be added, you can suggest it in the Glossary Requests thread here.
